audits.excerpt:
"Ranger-General," Vereesa said.

The formal title arrived before the sister did.
audits.reason: The second sentence explains the family wound from above instead of letting the title land through voice, timing, and face. This is exactly the kind of clean interpretive line the grounding pass should replace with bodily evidence already present in the scene.
audits.suggestedFix: "Ranger-General," Vereesa said. The title came out first, hard and formal, before the rest of her face caught up.

audits.excerpt: She folded the recall with slow care and held it at her side like something that had failed both of them.
audits.reason: The simile lifts the beat into portable commentary when the vellum, wax, and grip already carry the damage. The scene should stay with paper, hand, and road-spent body rather than naming the emotional verdict for the reader.
audits.suggestedFix: She folded the recall with slow care and lowered it to her thigh, thumb pressed over the softening wax.

audits.excerpt:
Good.

Better that way.
...
It had to stay that way.
audits.reason: This sequence explains the stone-call boundary in conceptual terms instead of keeping Sylvanas inside the actual sensory lane available to her. The passage is strongest when it stays with pulse-light, wind, horses, and posture rather than approving the rule from outside the moment.
audits.suggestedFix: Sylvanas kept her eyes on the amber pulse in Jaina's hand and the set of her shoulders. From here the call was only light, wind, and posture. That was enough.

audits.excerpt: Some of the fury left her face. Weariness took its place.
audits.reason: This swaps one emotional label for another instead of cashing the change out physically. Vereesa is already dusted, road-spent, and holding herself on duty; the prose should show the drop in her body rather than naming the interior state.
audits.suggestedFix: Vereesa's mouth loosened. Her shoulders dropped a notch under the road dust.

audits.reason: The line gives the reader a tidy interpretive summary after Jaina has already answered. The beat would land harder through the sound and rein-hand that follow the word instead of the narrator stepping in to gloss it.
audits.suggestedFix: The word came flat, no give in it, and the mare's bit clicked once when Jaina gathered the reins again.

audits.excerpt: Camp became absence in less than a minute: stamped earth, crushed grass, one cold patch of fire, then nothing.
audits.reason: "Became absence" names the effect abstractly after the concrete remnants already do the work. The line should trust the stamped earth, crushed grass, and dead fire to mark departure without stepping into thematic shorthand.
audits.suggestedFix: In less than a minute there was only stamped earth behind them, crushed grass, and one cold patch of fire where the camp had been.

audits.excerpt:
Open water ahead.

Certain anyway.
...
Ahead waited coast, failing relays, borrowed passage, and the prince they had not stopped in time.
Good.
audits.reason: The closing threshold drifts out of the horses, cold, and wind into thematic approval. The chapter should finish on embodied forward pressure, not on the narrator endorsing the pursuit.
audits.suggestedFix: Open water ahead. The wind off it laid salt on her lips and pulled colder through the gloves. Ahead waited coast, failing relays, borrowed passage, and the prince they had not stopped in time. Sylvanas bent lower into the wind and kept the mare moving.
